{"id":"project-LICHEN-h8hb","title":"app_identity: set_self_from_link_ctx reads link_ctx without synchronization","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/app_identity/app_identity.c:137-167

lichen_app_identity_set_self_from_link_ctx reads multiple fields from the link context without holding the context's seq_lock:
- ctx->has_key (line 147)
- ctx->eui64 (line 152)
- ctx->ed25519_pk (line 153)

While the function's doc comment warns callers to serialize with key operations, this is error-prone and easily violated.

Race scenario:
1. Thread A calls lichen_app_identity_set_self_from_link_ctx()
2. Thread A checks ctx->has_key (true) at line 147
3. Thread B calls lichen_link_cleanup() which:
   - Zeros ed25519_pk
   - Sets has_key = false
4. Thread A copies ctx->ed25519_pk (now zeroed) at line 153
5. Thread A publishes an identity with an all-zeros public key

Impact: A zeroed public key breaks Schnorr-48 signature verification. Other nodes may cache this invalid key via TOFU, permanently breaking communication.

The link context has a seq_lock mutex but it only protects epoch/sequence allocation, not key reads. The app_identity module should not rely on caller discipline for security-critical operations.

Suggested fix: Either:
1. Acquire ctx->seq_lock (or a new key_lock) before reading key material
2. Use atomic snapshot operations in link_ctx
3. Document that has_key/eui64/ed25519_pk are immutable after load (but they aren't - cleanup zeros them)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:40:24.30856Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:40:24.308593Z"}

2. Thread A checks ctx->has_key (true) at line 147
3. Thread B calls lichen_link_cleanup() which:
   - Zeros ed25519_pk
   - Sets has_key = false
4. Thread A copies ctx->ed25519_pk (now zeroed) at line 153
5. Thread A publishes an identity with an all-zeros public key

Impact: A zeroed public key breaks Schnorr-48 signature verification. Other nodes may cache this invalid key via TOFU, permanently breaking communication.

The link context has a seq_lock mutex but it only protects epoch/sequence allocation, not key reads. The app_identity module should not rely on caller discipline for security-critical operations.

Suggested fix: Either:
1. Acquire ctx->seq_lock (or a new key_lock) before reading key material
2. Use atomic snapshot operations in link_ctx
3. Document that has_key/eui64/ed25519_pk are immutable after load (but they aren't - cleanup zeros them)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:40:24.30856Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T20:17:11.227386321Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T20:17:11.227386321Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T20:22:11.227386321Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T20:17:11.227386321Z"}

new_value: Now uses lichen_link_copy_identity() for atomic snapshot of key material under lock. Prevents zeroed pubkey race with cleanup. Matches suggested fix #2. Verified in current implementation (e133541cc).
